text: The brassworks shift bell is suing the workers for making it ring during a strike. The bell is represented by the owner's barrister, who keeps asking the court to recognize his client as management.

choices.text: Help the workers elect a new schedule

outcomes.text: Every shift elects a timekeeper. The bell is retained as an employee, denied supervisory authority, and given Tuesdays off for polishing.

choices.text: Cross-examine the bell

success.text: You establish that the bell has never entered the factory, touched a hammer, or experienced time. The workers win on standing and excellent heckling.

fail.text: The barrister rings his client whenever you object. The judge mistakes volume for precedent.

choices.text: Accept management's quiet settlement

outcomes.text: The workers receive one paid holiday. The bell receives a corner office and begins scheduling mandatory silence.

text: An absentee count has reclassified the tenants of Crooked Court as ornamental houseplants. Rent now includes a sunlight surcharge, a watering fee, and a ban on unauthorized photosynthesis.

choices.text: Organize the entire building as a greenhouse

outcomes.text: The tenants register one enormous cooperative fern. The count cannot evict it without admitting plants deserve hearings, which would ruin his garden business.

choices.text: Prune the count's ownership records

success.text: By morning the deed lists every tenant as a root. The registry clerk approves it because the botanical seal is lovely.

fail.text: You are caught with the shears and billed for administrative topiary.

choices.text: Pay one family's surcharge

outcomes.text: One family stays this month. The count cites your payment as proof that the system contains generosity.

text: The League of Extremely Natural Hierarchy has a permit to march through the market. Its uniforms were paid for by the granary owners, whose names appear nowhere except every invoice.

choices.text: Build a broad blockade with the market workers

outcomes.text: Dockers, bakers, clerks, liberals with nervous signs, and three grandmothers hold the street. The league discovers that history can be stopped by people who disagree about nearly everything else.

choices.text: Redirect the parade into the manure festival

success.text: The league salutes six decorated carts before realizing the decorations are agricultural. Recruitment suffers. The cabbages remain politically neutral.

fail.text: The league notices the detour. You escape under a ceremonial turnip while the guards arrest the map.

choices.text: Expose the granary owners behind the uniforms

outcomes.text: The invoices circulate by supper. Respectable sponsors resign from organizations they insist they never joined.
effects: rumor 95 The granary owners financed the hierarchy league.

text: Two generals occupy opposite ends of a tavern table and refuse to surrender the salt. Between them, conscripted servers move mugs through a disputed neutral zone marked in gravy.

choices.text: Organize the soldiers and servers to fraternize

outcomes.text: The rank and file share the salt, then the bread, then several opinions about going home. Both generals declare a tactical shortage of troops.

choices.text: Steal both sets of battle orders

success.text: Tomorrow's offensive is delayed because each army receives instructions to seize the dessert cart.

fail.text: You steal the wine list. It is still the most humane document on the table.

choices.text: Negotiate peace for the salt cellar only

outcomes.text: The salt is demilitarized. The villages remain scheduled for dawn, but everyone praises the achievable scope.

text: Survivors reach the border carrying a handwritten registry of everyone from their valley. The clerk says the names cannot be entered because official ink for that valley was discontinued when officials decided the valley was empty.

choices.text: Copy every name into the public archive

outcomes.text: Scribes work through the night. The state can still deny the crime, but now it must deny each person individually and in triplicate.
effects: rumor 100 The valley survivors placed every stolen name in the public archive.

choices.text: Guide the families around the checkpoint

success.text: Everyone crosses by an old shepherd path. The registry arrives folded inside a child's coat.

fail.text: The patrol finds the path. You buy enough time for most families, and not enough time to pretend most means all.

choices.text: Force the clerk to read the registry aloud

outcomes.text: The queue listens until sunset. Procedure does not become justice, but it can no longer claim ignorance.

text: A refinery has begun selling weather forecasts for the smoke cloud it produces. Premium subscribers learn which streets will cough tomorrow. Everyone else is advised to enjoy the mystery.

choices.text: Help residents shut the smokestacks

outcomes.text: The furnaces cool. Workers keep the maintenance lights on and begin converting the yard to clean power before the owner returns from someplace with better air.

choices.text: Publish the forecast for free

success.text: The subscription collapses. So does the fiction that uncertainty, rather than poison, was the problem.

fail.text: You transpose east and west. The refinery cites your mistake as peer review of smoke.

choices.text: Buy tomorrow's cleanest route

outcomes.text: You breathe easier for one day. The forecast calls your purchase consumer-led environmentalism.

text: The clinic staff are striking for medicine, sleep, and enough chairs. The patients join the line because the clinic owner has repossessed the waiting room while several people were still waiting in it.

choices.text: Turn the picket into a street clinic

outcomes.text: Nurses treat patients, patients guard the supplies, and neighbors bring chairs. The owner calls this theft of a business model.

choices.text: Buy the clinic one emergency supply crate

outcomes.text: The crate saves lives this week. The owner adds Emergency Generosity to next month's bill.

choices.text: Steal the owner's chair collection

success.text: The waiting room becomes magnificent. One velvet chair is diagnosed with moral emptiness and placed near a window.

fail.text: A chair catches you. It has wheels and the instincts of a guard dog.

text: A dragon has cornered the bread-futures market. It owns no bread, only contracts promising bread later, and sleeps on them because paper is cooler than gold and less likely to attract heroes.

choices.text: Open the granaries with the bakers

outcomes.text: Bread reaches the streets. The dragon's contracts become napkins, which is their first practical use.

choices.text: Convince the dragon to eat its portfolio

success.text: You describe the contracts as artisanal fiber. The dragon consumes a controlling interest and develops excellent regularity.

fail.text: The dragon recognizes financial advice and charges a consultation fee.

choices.text: Buy one profitable loaf contract

outcomes.text: Your paper loaf appreciates beautifully while an actual bakery closes within smelling distance.

text: The museum's prized ceremonial door has unhinged itself and started walking home. The curator follows with a net, shouting that the door cannot leave because it represents universal heritage.

choices.text: Escort the door back to its community

outcomes.text: The door arrives to a crowd that remembers the frame. It opens onto a house the museum catalog described as extinct.
effects: rumor 95 The museum door walked home and found its family.

choices.text: Make the curator prove ownership to the door

success.text: The deed says acquired during a period of educational smoke. The door slams once, conclusively.

fail.text: The curator produces twelve forms and one portrait of himself near the door. Procedure becomes briefly impassable.

choices.text: Sell tickets to the escape

outcomes.text: The crowd cheers the door and pays you. The museum trademarks Walking Home before sunset.

text: Vulture financiers attend the funeral of a failed cooperative carrying appraisal hooks. They plan to buy the ovens, sell the recipes, and lease the workers their own aprons.

choices.text: Help the workers buy everything for one coin

outcomes.text: Every financier bids against an empty chair while the workers purchase the lot under the category sentimental debris.

choices.text: Auction the financiers' own contracts

success.text: Their agreements allow transfer of distressed assets. You establish that the financiers are visibly distressed.

fail.text: The fine print bites. Literally. One contract has teeth and a preferred creditor.

choices.text: Take a private fee to value the ovens low

outcomes.text: You are paid well. The ovens are sold cheaply to a holding company that owns the person who paid you.

text: A starship fabricator can make unlimited soup. A merchant has attached a meter that charges for heat, bowl depth, aroma, and exposure to the concept of soup.

choices.text: Remove the meter and publish the repair manual

outcomes.text: The machine feeds the district. Its first free bowl tastes faintly of tomato and unauthorized possibility.

choices.text: Ask the machine who installed scarcity

outcomes.text: It displays seventeen centuries of ownership changes and one recurring surname. The merchant develops an urgent appointment elsewhere.
effects: rumor 90 The soup meter's owners have been selling artificial scarcity for centuries.

choices.text: Buy exclusive rights to the smell

outcomes.text: You become wealthy whenever somebody nearby remembers lunch. The hungry are offered nose plugs on credit.

text: Floodwater reaches the council chamber while the Emergency Umbrella Subcommittee debates whether its resolution requires a semicolon. The umbrella manufacturers have asked everyone to avoid divisive drainage.

choices.text: Open the drains without unanimous consent

outcomes.text: The district drains. The minutes record a grave breach of procedure and, in smaller type, several thousand saved homes.

choices.text: Build a majority for immediate action

success.text: Enough cautious members become briefly brave together. The resolution passes with the semicolon abstaining.

fail.text: You win the argument after the water wins the ground floor.

choices.text: Accept a pilot drain in the wealthy quarter

outcomes.text: The pilot succeeds magnificently by moving the flood to everyone excluded from the pilot.

text: A guard has arrested a dockworker's shadow for assembling without its body. The dockworker must report to work at noon, when the evidence becomes impossible to conceal.

choices.text: Gather everyone's shadows at the jail

outcomes.text: By sunset the cells contain half the city and none of the people. The guards release the evidence because it refuses meals.

choices.text: Argue that the shadow lacks legal personhood

success.text: The court releases it and immediately taxes it as property. Freedom arrives with an invoice.

fail.text: The judge grants personhood solely for sentencing. The shadow looks darker somehow.

choices.text: Bribe the guard at sundown

outcomes.text: The shadow slips away. The guard starts a private detention service for silhouettes.

text: The dungeon monsters have formed Local 404. Their demands include safer lairs, predictable hero hours, and an end to treasure chests impersonating nonunion furniture. A mimic serves as shop steward and several chairs look nervous.

choices.text: Recognize the local and bargain honestly

outcomes.text: Adventuring continues by appointment. Casualties fall, treasure is audited, and mimics receive meal breaks that are also just meals.

choices.text: Help write the grievance against heroes

outcomes.text: The document is persuasive until the gelatinous cube signs across every page at once.

choices.text: Offer management a replacement monster crew

outcomes.text: The replacement monsters were told this was theater work. They join the line after the first spear trap.

text: Veterans rehearse last year's failed uprising as street theater. The cardboard barricade is better organized than the original, and the audience has begun handing the actors real tools.

choices.text: Turn the rehearsal into an assembly

outcomes.text: The crowd studies the failure without worshiping it. By dusk there is no revolution, only committees, supply lists, and the frightening beginning of capacity.

choices.text: Play the official who changes sides too late

outcomes.text: Your performance receives uneasy laughter from six actual officials. Two stay afterward to ask when their scene begins.
effects: rumor 80 The failed uprising returned as a rehearsal for the next attempt.

choices.text: Sell commemorative cobblestones

outcomes.text: The stones sell out. The veterans discover their old barricade is now a premium lifestyle material.

text: A free ship rewrites its constitution during a storm. The captain wants emergency powers, the crew wants emergency soup, and the mainmast has submitted a written objection by falling across the ballot box.

choices.text: Give every worker one vote, including the cook

outcomes.text: The cook's storm plan wins by a landslide and a ladle. The ship survives under temporary soup authority, which expires exactly on time.

choices.text: Grant the captain power until the mast is upright

outcomes.text: The mast rises. The captain argues the emergency continues because the horizon remains suspiciously wet.

choices.text: Let the mast cast the deciding vote

outcomes.text: Its position is interpreted as a firm objection to concentrated power and weak rope maintenance.

text: An impossible six-legged walking hedge steps from the reeds and asks why discovery always means the discoverer owns what was discovered. Behind you, three natural philosophers quietly hide a measuring cage.

choices.text: Listen before naming anything

outcomes.text: It speaks about seasons, fear, and the exhausting human need to put brass labels on neighbors. Then it teaches you where the poisoned water begins.

choices.text: Publish its account with its consent

success.text: The paper lists the hedge as coauthor. Academia objects until it answers every objection in pollen.

fail.text: You accidentally call it a specimen in the introduction. It edits the word into colleague and circles it six times.

choices.text: Help the philosophers close the cage

outcomes.text: The hedge walks through the bars, which were designed by experts in things that do not walk through bars. It remembers you accurately.

text: A mother has brought soup to the demon her child accidentally summoned. She asks whether it is warm enough, whether the abyss is drafty, and why the summoners have not provided a chair. The demon, eight feet of flame and embarrassment, admits this is the first time anyone asked.

choices.text: Seat them both at the workers' table

outcomes.text: The demon learns that personhood includes soup, boundaries, and helping stack chairs. By midnight it is organizing summoned labor against unpaid eternity.

choices.text: Help the demon file a grievance against its summoners

success.text: The contract promised terror but omitted travel, meals, and fireproof seating. The demon wins back pay and sends its mother a thank-you card despite having met her once.

fail.text: The court rules infernal beings are equipment. The mother asks whether the judge would say that to its face. The judge schedules reconsideration.

choices.text: Sell holy water to the frightened patrons

outcomes.text: The demon checks the label and identifies ordinary tap water. Its mother buys one anyway because you look underfed, which is devastating.

text: The Ministry of Public Reassurance distributes pamphlets denying that the Ministry of Public Reassurance exists. Each pamphlet includes office hours, a donation envelope, and directions to the denial desk.

choices.text: Print workers' testimony on the blank backs

outcomes.text: The official lie travels through town carrying unofficial truth. The ministry recalls every pamphlet, thereby confirming its address.
effects: rumor 95 The denial pamphlets carried workers' testimony on the reverse.

choices.text: Visit the denial desk and request proof

success.text: The clerk stamps your form MINISTRY ABSENT using the ministry seal. You applaud until the queue understands.

fail.text: You are redirected to an office that denies the denial desk. It has better chairs.

choices.text: Take the ministry's advertising contract

outcomes.text: You are paid to increase public awareness of institutional nonexistence. Recognition reaches ninety percent.

text: Villagers repair a washed-out bridge for travelers they will never meet. The toll company orders them to stop performing unauthorized rescue because free passage could create expectations.

choices.text: Carry stone with the villagers

outcomes.text: The bridge opens at dusk. Nobody earns a profit, hundreds cross safely, and the company records a catastrophic failure of incentives.

choices.text: Buy the company enough time to look away

success.text: You schedule a ribbon-cutting seminar upstream. By the time management returns, the bridge has become a fact.

fail.text: Management arrives early and confiscates the ribbon. The villagers keep building with less festive materials.

choices.text: Pay the toll for the next ten strangers

outcomes.text: Ten people cross. The company raises the toll after observing demand. Your kindness mattered, and the trap learned from it.
